name: extract_readable
description: Convert raw HTML into clean markdown plus a list of links. Uses cheerio + turndown locally — no network.
hints: Use after fetch_url when the response body is raw HTML and you want LLM-friendly markdown

name: fetch_url
description: HTTP GET a URL with a timeout and size cap. Returns status, content-type, and body text (truncated to 500KB).
hints: Use for raw HTTP fetches when you need the unmodified response body
Prefer read_url when you want clean markdown instead of raw HTML

name: read_url
description: Fetch a URL and return LLM-ready markdown. Uses Jina Reader (https://r.jina.ai/<url>) by default — no API key needed. Falls back to local fetch_url + extract_readable if Jina fails.
hints: Default choice for reading a web page — returns clean markdown
Use this instead of fetch_url when you want content you can summarize directly
Automatically falls back to local extraction if Jina Reader is unreachable

name: search_web
description: Search the web via a tiered provider chain. Default: DuckDuckGo (keyless). Upgrades automatically if TAVILY_API_KEY, EXA_API_KEY, BRAVE_API_KEY, PERPLEXITY_API_KEY, FIRECRAWL_API_KEY, or JINA_API_KEY is set. Pass `provider` to override.
hints: Primary tool for finding sources on any web topic
Returns which provider served the result so you can weight result quality
Use provider='exa' for semantic 'find pages like X' search, 'perplexity' when you want an answer + citations
